articles.title: Coffee and Tea Help Keep Your Brain Healthy
articles.summary: A big study that lasted for 43 years found that drinking coffee or tea can help protect your brain. Scientists looked at over 130,000 people and discovered that those who drank 2-3 cups of coffee or 1-2 cups of tea each day had a lower chance of getting dementia, which is a brain disease that makes it hard to remember things. The study showed that these drinks might help keep your brain working well as you get older. The researchers think that caffeine in coffee and tea could be the reason for this benefit. They also found that it doesn't matter if you have a family history of dementia; coffee and tea can still help. This is good news because there aren't many ways to prevent dementia, so drinking these beverages might be a simple thing you can do. The study was done by doctors from big hospitals and schools like Harvard, and they used data from long-term health studies to get these results.

articles.title: Pink Rocks Help Find a Hidden Giant Under Antarctica
articles.summary: In Antarctica, scientists found bright pink rocks on mountains that led them to a big discovery. Under a glacier called Pine Island Glacier, there is a huge hidden rock made of granite. It is very old, from the time of dinosaurs about 175 million years ago. The rocks on the surface came from this hidden giant when the ice was thicker and moved differently long ago. This helps scientists learn how the ice sheet behaved in the past and how it might change in the future. Understanding this can help predict sea level rise because melting ice in Antarctica affects oceans worldwide. The study used airplanes to measure gravity and find the hidden rock.

articles.title: Dinosaurs with Wings That Couldn't Fly
articles.summary: Scientists found dinosaur fossils with feathers that show these animals had wings but could not fly. The study looked at fossils from China that are 160 million years old. The feathers were preserved with colors, which is very rare. By studying how the feathers grew and were replaced, called molting, researchers learned that these dinosaurs did not fly. This tells us that flight in dinosaurs and birds evolved in a complex way, and some species might have lost the ability to fly over time. The research helps us understand more about ancient animals and how they lived.

articles.title: AI Energy Use: Not a Big Problem for the Planet
articles.summary: This article talks about how artificial intelligence (AI) uses electricity, but it might not be as bad for the environment as some people think. Researchers studied AI's energy use in the United States and found it is about the same as all the energy used in Iceland. Even though that sounds like a lot, it is too small to make a big difference in global pollution. The scientists say that AI could even help fight climate change by creating new green technologies. They looked at different jobs and industries to see how AI is used. The study shows that while AI uses energy, its overall impact on the planet is not very big, and it might have benefits too.

articles.title: A Bright Fireball in the Sky Over Ohio and Pennsylvania
articles.summary: On Tuesday morning, people in Ohio and Pennsylvania saw a bright fireball and white streaks in the sky. It was likely a meteor, which is a space rock that burns up as it enters Earth's atmosphere. The National Weather Service and NASA confirmed that the fireball fell near Cleveland in Ohio. It made a loud boom sound because it broke the sound barrier when it landed. No one was hurt, and it was just a natural event from space. This is exciting because meteors are rare to see during the day. Scientists study meteors to learn more about our solar system. The fireball was spotted by many people and reported online, showing how we can share news quickly.

articles.title: A Lawsuit to Save a Weather and Climate Research Center
articles.summary: A group that runs a big science center called the National Center for Atmospheric Research (NCAR) is suing the Trump administration. The government wants to shut down NCAR, which studies weather, climate, and space weather. NCAR is in Boulder, Colorado, and has supercomputers and airplanes for research. Many scientists use it to learn about the atmosphere. The lawsuit says the government is closing NCAR without a good reason, just because President Trump is mad at Colorado. It also says the government broke rules by not explaining why and by telling people not to talk about it. The group hopes the lawsuit will stop the shutdown and save this important research center.
